title: AICBG (anterior iliac crest bone graft)
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: AICBG (anterior iliac crest bone graft)

Attention was directed to the aicbg:side:0 anterior hip, prepped and draped as a separate sterile field with care taken not to cross-contaminate the recipient site. A cutaneous and subcutaneous incision was made with a #15 blade along the aicbg:side:1 anterior iliac crest, 2.0 cm posterior and lateral to the anterior superior iliac spine, measuring approximately aicbg:measurement:0 cm. Electrocautery carried the incision down to periosteum through Scarpa's fascia and the investing fascia of the external oblique and tensor fascia lata, taking care not to dissect off the tensor fascia lata. The iliac crest periosteum was incised and subperiosteal dissection performed along the length of the incision and medially down the anterior iliac crest. Malleable and Cobra retractors were placed to protect the abdominal contents. Using pre-planned segments to template the graft, a reciprocating saw made the vertical osteotomies and a piezotome the horizontal osteotomy under copious irrigation, and chisel osteotomes were used to free the tricortical block. Spoon curettes were then used to harvest medullary bone from the anterior iliac crest. The harvest site was irrigated thoroughly, packed with Arista and thrombin-soaked Gelfoam, and hemostasis confirmed. The hip was closed in layers, with 3-0 Vicryl approximating periosteum and muscular aponeurosis, 4-0 Vicryl for deep dermal closure, and running subcuticular skin closure reinforced with tacking 5-0 plain gut. Dermabond was applied and an island gauze and Tegaderm pressure dressing placed. At the recipient site, the bony margins were smoothed and the tricortical block contoured for passive fit, then fixated with aicbg:toothNumber:0 monocortical screws and a trimmed aicbg:text:0 titanium mesh. The harvested medullary bone was packed along the defect mixed with PRF. A aicbg:enumText:0 membrane was draped over the graft, and the flap was closed tension-free with horizontal mattress and interrupted 3-0 dyed Monocryl sutures after subperiosteal releasing incisions.

title: BSSO (Bilateral Sagittal Split Osteotomy)
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: BSSO (Bilateral Sagittal Split Osteotomy)

Attention was directed to the mandible. The mandibular surgical sites were anesthetized with 2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine by local infiltration and bilateral inferior alveolar and lingual nerve blocks. Attention was directed first to the right mandible, where bovie electrocautery was used to make a mucosal incision along the external oblique ridge extending from the ascending ramus to the gingival cuff near the first premolar. Dissection was carried to the bony mandible and a subperiosteal plane was developed along the lateral cortex and the ascending ramus with a #9 molt periosteal elevator. The temporalis tendon was stripped using a notched ramus retractor, and the masseter was reflected to allow placement of an inferior border retractor. A subperiosteal plane was developed on the medial ramus and the lingula and sigmoid notch were identified. The right inferior alveolar nerve was identified and protected with a retractor. A medial horizontal osteotomy was made just beyond the lingula with a reciprocating saw under copious irrigation, carried to the sagittal plane along the ascending ramus and monocortically through the lateral body. A piezosurgery unit was used to create the inferior border osteotomy and a monocortical oblique osteotomy joining the medial cut. The osteotomies were recapitulated with thin straight and curved osteotomes, then completed with a Smith spreader and inferior border spreader used serially until the segments split. The right inferior alveolar nerve was confirmed visualized completely intact and housed within the distal segment, and was gently dissected free of the proximal segment to ensure passivity, removing overlying bony canal as needed. Independent movement of the proximal and distal segments was verified and any proximal interferences were removed with a pineapple bur under irrigation, protecting the IAN. An identical incision, dissection, and osteotomy were performed on the left mandible, with the left inferior alveolar nerve likewise identified, dissected free, and confirmed intact within the distal segment. The patient was placed into maxillomandibular fixation with 24-gauge fish-loop wires and the final splint, seated without interferences. The proximal segments were reduced with a notched periosteal elevator, seating the condyles in the glenoid fossae with the inferior borders of the proximal and distal segments aligned, and passive fit was confirmed. Each side was fixated with a KLS bsso:hardwareCount:0-hole, bsso:text:0 dog bone plate using bsso:hardwareCount:1 bsso:measurement:0-mm monocortical screws to the proximal segment and bsso:hardwareCount:2 bsso:measurement:1-mm monocortical screws to the distal segment. Maxillomandibular fixation was released and the occlusion was confirmed to seat passively, reproducibly, and stably into the final splint. After copious irrigation, the incisions were closed with interrupted 4-0 Vicryl and running locking 3-0 chromic gut. The patient was placed into bilateral class II guiding elastics.

title: Canthopexy
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Canthopexy

Attention was drawn to the lateral right upper eyelid. canthopexy:measurement:0 cc of 2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine was injected. A lateral upper eyelid incision approximately 1 cm in length was made to approach the lateral orbital rim, and the superior lateral orbital rim was identified through this approach. While protecting the orbital contents, a wire-passing bur was used to create a transosseous drill hole through the lateral orbital rim. The lateral canthal tendon was identified and canthopexy was performed using 3-0 Prolene suture in a superior vector. Good lift was noted and the procedure was repeated on the contralateral side with symmetry noted. The site was closed with 6-0 plain gut to approximate the orbicularis oculi followed by running 6-0 plain gut for skin closure.

title: Coronectomy
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Coronectomy

Attention was directed to the coronectomy:side:0 coronectomy:enumText:0 vestibule. 10 cc of 1% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epi was infiltrated locally and an IAN block was performed. Using a #15 blade, a distal hockey stick incision was made to access coronectomy:toothNumber:0 and carried forward in a sulcular fashion to the adjacent tooth. A full thickness mucoperiosteal flap was raised with a periosteal elevator to expose the crown and surrounding crestal bone. A 702 bur under copious irrigation was used to section the crown from the root complex at the level of the alveolar crest, taking care not to mobilize the roots so as to protect the adjacent inferior alveolar nerve. The crown was delivered and the residual root surface was reduced 2 to 3 mm below the crestal bone with a round bur. The root fragment was confirmed immobile and the enamel was fully removed. The site was irrigated with sterile saline and inspected for hemostasis. The flap was repositioned and closed primarily over the retained root with 3-0 Vicryl suture.

title: Endoscopic Brow Lift
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Endoscopic Brow Lift

Lidocaine 2% with 1:100,000 epinephrine endoscopic-brow-lift:measurement:0 mL was given to the supraorbital, lateral orbital, and zygomatic regions. A marking pen was used to make five markings one inch posterior to the hairline: a vertical midline marking, two vertical paramedian markings, and two transverse temporal markings within the nasal ala-lateral canthus vector, each measuring about 3 cm in length. A #15 blade was used to make full-thickness incisions from skin to bone at the midline and paramedian regions. Dissection was carried deep to pericranium along the forehead, between the superior temporal lines, in a subperiosteal fashion using a #9 molt elevator and beavertail elevator. Using a downknuckle elevator, dissection was carried subperiosteally along the supraorbital rims, elevating the periosteum off the rims and then along the lateral orbital rims. A squid elevator was used to dissect subperiosteally below the zygomaticofrontal suture to the inferior orbital rim. Careful attention was made along the medial supraorbital rims to stop 2 cm superior to the rim and avoid damage to the supraorbital and supratrochlear nerves. A 30 degree rigid endoscope was inserted in the midline incision and advanced to the supraorbital rim region. A downknuckle elevator was placed in the paramedian regions to dissect subperiosteally around the supratrochlear and supraorbital nerves. Using the upknuckle elevator through the paramedian incision, the periosteum underneath the brows was scored and separated bilaterally until 1 cm of periosteum was elevated from the orbital rim. Incisions were made through skin at the temporal markings and dissection was carried through the temporoparietal fascia down to the temporalis fascia, with bleeding controlled by Bovie. Dissection along the temporalis fascial plane was carried laterally, posteriorly, and anteriorly, then medially through the superior temporal lines to connect with the subperiosteal forehead dissection. Temporal fixation was completed using 2-0 Ethibond sutures through the temporoparietal fascia affixed to the temporalis fascia in a vector following the nasal ala-lateral canthus line, one suture passed and tied on each side while the assistant superiorly elevated the lateral forehead. Osteotomies for the Endotines were prepared at the paramedian incisions and endoscopic-brow-lift:text:0 Endotines placed. The brows were elevated, the tissues allowed to stretch, and then re-draped on the Endotines at completion of the case. All incisions within the hairline were closed with staples. Adequate brow lift was appreciated with good symmetry.

title: Facelift (rhytidectomy)
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Facelift (rhytidectomy)

Tumescent solution, prepared by combining 50 cc of 2% lidocaine, 1 mg epinephrine (1 cc of 1:1,000), and 1 g tranexamic acid in 500 cc of 0.9% normal saline, was injected into the subcutaneous plane of the face and neck (~facelift-rhytidectomy:measurement:0 cc). Attention was first directed to the right face, where a #15 scalpel was used to make a trilobe pre-auricular incision with an anterior curvilinear temporal tuft extension. This incision was continued infra-lobularly, then post-auricularly to the height of the intersection of the pinna with the hairline, and carried into the hairline with a gentle curve. Electrocautery was used to establish a subcutaneous/supra-SMAS dissection plane pre- and post-auricularly, and facelift scissors completed the subcutaneous dissection. All access points were connected pre-to-post auricularly to develop a continuous subcutaneous skin flap with a smooth carpet of fat along the underside, with all bleeding controlled by electrocautery. The incision and subcutaneous dissection were then repeated on the contralateral face. A ~3 cm incision through SMAS was made with electrocautery along the trajectory of a line from the angle of the mandible to the lateral canthus, 1 cm inferior to the zygomatic arch and 1 cm anterior to the tragus. A sub-SMAS plane was developed to near the anterior border of the parotid, then extended beyond it with blunt vertical spreading until sufficient traction could be applied to the SMAS to achieve the desired lift. The mandibular retaining ligament and masseteric cutaneous ligaments were sharply released. The SMAS was secured in a superior position with 2-0 Vicryl followed by gentle lateral traction and securing 2-0 Vicryl sutures, then a running 2-0 Vicryl smoothed the SMAS surface and corrected standing cutaneous deformities. The SMAS work was repeated on the contralateral face. Circumferential undermining was performed as indicated to allow rotation of the skin flap with control of standing cutaneous deformities. Tisseel fibrin sealant was applied to the underside of the flap. The skin flaps were redraped, excess skin was excised stepwise, and the wound edges were secured with 4-0 monocryl deep dermal stay sutures along the temporal tuft, pre-auricular, and post-auricular incisions, with staples within the hairline and a running 6-0 plain gut epidermal layer. A beveled earlobe wedge was excised as needed to create a curvilinear face-to-lobule transition with passive approximation. Hemostasis was confirmed throughout.

title: Genioplasty (sliding)
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Genioplasty (sliding)

Attention was directed to the anterior mandible. Local anesthetic consisting of 1% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine was infiltrated into the anterior mandibular vestibule. An incision was made with bovie electrocautery from canine to canine, well below the mucogingival junction, leaving an adequate mucosal cuff for closure. Dissection was carried down through the mentalis muscle to the symphysis, and the mental nerves were identified and protected bilaterally. A horizontal sliding osteotomy was marked and performed with a reciprocating saw under copious irrigation, kept at least 5 mm below the apices of the teeth and below the mental foramina, accounting for the anterior loop of the inferior alveolar nerve. The inferior segment was mobilized and advanced genioplasty-sliding:measurement:0 mm to the planned position and confirmed symmetric in the midline. It was fixated rigidly with a pre-bent titanium chin plate and monocortical screws. After irrigation, the incision was closed in layers with 3-0 Vicryl to reapproximate the mentalis muscle and 3-0 chromic gut for the mucosa.

title: Hardware Removal
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Hardware Removal

The maxillary surgical sites were anesthetized with lidocaine 2% with 1:100,000 epinephrine via local infiltration. Attention was directed to the maxillary buccal vestibule, where a circumvestibular incision was initiated with Bovie electrocautery starting at the mucosa of the hardware-removal:side:0 first molar approximately 5 mm superior to the mucogingival junction to the contralateral first molar, following the prior incision line, and carried to bone. Periosteal elevators were used to reflect a full-thickness mucoperiosteal flap to expose the maxillary hardware extending from buttress to buttress. The flap was extended superiorly to expose the piriform rims and the infraorbital neurovascular bundles bilaterally, both of which were noted intact and protected throughout the case. With subperiosteal dissection complete, any screws encased in or reflected within soft tissue were identified, exposed with a #15 blade stab incision as needed, and retrieved with hemostats. Where the hardware was partially osseointegrated with bony coverage at the piriform rims and osteotomy junctions, a #2 round diamond bur under copious irrigation along with osteotomes was used to completely expose the hardware. All screws were removed and accounted for using a universal driver, with a mallet used to seat the driver as needed. Any sheared screw threads were removed with a needle driver. If purulence was encountered, the site was thoroughly irrigated and care taken to completely express it. The surgical site was again thoroughly irrigated with copious sterile saline. The circumvestibular incision was closed with a running 3-0 Vicryl suture. The oropharynx was irrigated and suctioned free of debris.

title: Incision and Drainage
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Incision and Drainage

Attention was directed to the incision-and-drainage:side:0 submandibular region. An incision was planned greater than incision-and-drainage:measurement:0 cm below the inferior border of the mandible to protect the marginal mandibular branch of the facial nerve, and 4 cc of 1% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine was infiltrated in a subdermal plane. A incision-and-drainage:measurement:1 cm incision was made with a #15 blade at the planned site, carried through dermis, subcutaneous fat, platysma, and underlying fascia. Blunt dissection was carried out with a curved hemostat in the closed position down to the inferior border of the mandible, then opened to explore the involved space. The fluid collection on the medial surface of the posterior mandible was entered and drainage expressed. The drainage was sampled on a sterile cotton-tipped applicator and sent for Gram stain and aerobic and anaerobic culture and sensitivity. The space was thoroughly irrigated with sterile saline. A 0.25 inch Penrose drain was placed into the dependent portion of the wound and secured to the skin with a 2-0 nylon suture. A sterile 4x4 dressing was placed over the drain. The site was hemostatic with minimal drainage.

title: Laser Skin Resurfacing (CO2)
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Laser Skin Resurfacing (CO2)

External eye shields were worn by the patient during the entire procedure, and wavelength specific eye protection was worn by all staff in the room. Mirrors were stowed and opaque film applied to the windows. The vacuum was used for smoke evacuation, laser masks were worn by all staff, fire extinguishers were on standby, and a wet gauze/towel was on the field. Using the Lumenis UltraPulse CO2 laser (10,600 nm) with the DeepFX handpiece, ablative fractional treatment was delivered to the laser-skin-resurfacing-co2:optionalClause:0 at laser-skin-resurfacing-co2:measurement:0 mJ, laser-skin-resurfacing-co2:measurement:1 Hz, laser-skin-resurfacing-co2:measurement:2% density, with pulses delivered in rows with minimal overlap. The ActiveFX handpiece was then used to deliver a second pass to the laser-skin-resurfacing-co2:optionalClause:1 at laser-skin-resurfacing-co2:measurement:3 mJ for the periorbital area and laser-skin-resurfacing-co2:measurement:4 mJ for the remainder of the face, laser-skin-resurfacing-co2:measurement:5 Hz, laser-skin-resurfacing-co2:measurement:6% density, again in rows with minimal overlap. Hemostasis was confirmed following laser treatment and Aquaphor was applied to the entire treated area at the conclusion of the procedure.

PROCEDURE: Le Fort I Osteotomy

Attention was directed to the maxilla. Local anesthetic consisting of 1% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine was infiltrated into the maxillary buccal vestibule. A circumvestibular incision was made with a needle-point bovie from first molar to first molar approximately 5 mm superior to the mucogingival junction and carried down to bone. A full-thickness mucoperiosteal flap was reflected with periosteal elevators, exposing the maxilla from the piriform rims to the pterygomaxillary junctions and superiorly to the right and left infraorbital neurovascular bundles, both of which were identified and protected and remained intact for the duration of the case. The nasal mucosa was elevated off the floor and lateral nasal walls with a curved free elevator and a Sayer elevator bilaterally. The planned Le Fort I osteotomy was marked and completed bilaterally from the maxillary buttress to the piriform rim using a reciprocating saw and piezosurgery unit, staying clear of the tooth roots. The nasal septum was separated from the palate with a double safe-sided osteotome and the lateral nasal walls were separated with single safe-sided osteotomes. The pterygomaxillary junctions were separated, and the maxilla was down-fractured with manual pressure into a freely mobile position. The descending palatine vessels were preserved. Bony interferences were removed to allow passive seating. The maxilla was advanced le-fort-i-osteotomy:measurement:0 mm and impacted le-fort-i-osteotomy:measurement:1 mm into the planned position per the intermediate splint with the patient in maxillomandibular fixation, then secured with four L-shaped titanium miniplates and monocortical screws. Maxillomandibular fixation was released and the intermediate splint was removed. An alar cinch suture was placed with 2-0 PDS. After copious irrigation, the maxillary incision was closed in a V-Y fashion with 3-0 chromic gut.

title: Lower Blepharoplasty (transconjunctival, fat repositioning)
sourcePath: Note Templates/Procedures/Cosmetic & Facial/Lower Blepharoplasty (transconjunctival, fat repositioning).md
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Lower Blepharoplasty (transconjunctival, fat repositioning)

Corneal shields were placed with ophthalmic lubrication. A Colorado Bovie tip was used to make a transconjunctival incision and expose the inferior orbital fat pads bilaterally. The inferior orbital rim arcus marginalis was released with bovie and periosteal elevators, and subperiosteal dissection was carried inferior to the orbital rim. The lateral and central orbital fat pads were identified and mobilized. The infraorbital nerves were visualized bilaterally and protected. Using 4-0 monocryl suture, the fat was repositioned by passing the suture from the lateral and central fat pads medially to exit through the skin of the melolabial fold near the ala. The suture was then passed back into the skin near the ala, creating a loop. A bolster dressing was placed under this loop of suture to provide additional support and prevent skin dimpling. The suture was then passed back laterally into the central and lateral fat pads and tied, securing the repositioned fat in its new position to address the tear trough deformity and provide midface volume restoration. This technique was repeated bilaterally with careful attention to symmetry. Hemostasis was confirmed. No excess orbital fat was excised and no skin excision was performed given the transconjunctival approach.

title: Mandibular Tori Removal
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Mandibular Tori Removal

Attention was directed to the mandibular-tori-removal:side:0 mandible. 10 cc of 1% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epi was infiltrated into the lingual mucosa, achieving hydrodissection over the torus, and an IAN block was performed. A lingual sulcular incision was made with a #15 blade from the first molar to the midline and a full thickness mucoperiosteal lingual flap was raised to expose the torus. A lingual trough osteotomy was initiated with a 701 bur under copious irrigation, mandibular-tori-removal:measurement:0 mm from the adjacent teeth, establishing a cleavage plane for torus removal. With a malleable retractor protecting the floor of the mouth, a T-handle osteotome was used to complete the osteotomy and remove the torus. Sharp corners of bone were smoothed with a hand rasp. The floor of the mouth and Wharton's duct were confirmed intact. The site was irrigated thoroughly with sterile saline, hemostasis was ensured, and the flap was closed primarily with interrupted 3-0 chromic gut suture.

title: Midface Lift
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Midface Lift

Tumescent solution, prepared by combining 50 cc of 2% lidocaine, 1 mg epinephrine (1 cc of 1:1,000), and 1 g tranexamic acid in 500 cc of 0.9% normal saline, was infiltrated into the midface dissection planes (~midface-lift:measurement:0 cc). Attention was directed to the right midface. Through a temporal and lateral orbital approach, dissection was carried subperiosteally below the zygomaticofrontal suture along the lateral orbital rim and across the body of the zygoma to the inferior orbital rim using a squid and beavertail elevator. Careful subperiosteal release of the midface soft tissues over the malar eminence was continued until the malar fat pad and midface complex were adequately mobilized, with care taken to protect the infraorbital nerve. The released midface tissues were elevated in a superolateral vector and suspended to the deep temporalis fascia with midface-lift:measurement:1-0 midface-lift:text:0 suture under appropriate tension to restore malar projection and efface the nasojugal groove. Symmetry was confirmed against the contralateral side and the identical procedure was performed on the opposite midface. Hemostasis was confirmed throughout. The access incisions were closed in layers.

title: MMF / Closed Reduction
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: MMF / Closed Reduction

The oral cavity was suctioned free of secretions and debris and a throat pack was placed. 10 cc of 1% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epi was infiltrated into the maxillary and mandibular vestibules. Using 24-gauge wire, a bridle wire was placed from tooth mmf-closed-reduction:toothNumber:0 to level the mandibular occlusion. Maxillary and mandibular arch bars were placed with 24-gauge wire from second premolar to second premolar. Alternatively, mmf-closed-reduction:hardwareCount:0 mmf-closed-reduction:measurement:0-mm IMF screws were placed into the mandible and maxilla with even distribution. The fractures were reduced and the arches placed tightly into maxillomandibular fixation (MMF) with 24-gauge wires looped between fixation points. Occlusion was thoroughly evaluated and verified to be native, stable, and repeatable. The fracture reduction was confirmed satisfactory. The throat pack was removed and the oropharynx irrigated and suctioned free of debris. The patient was placed in guiding elastics.

title: Nasal Bone Reduction (closed)
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Nasal Bone Reduction (closed)

Attention was directed to the nasal cavity and nose. Afrin-soaked pledgets were placed in both nostrils while local anesthesia was administered. Bilateral infraorbital and supratrochlear blocks were performed with 10 cc of 1% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine, and additional local anesthetic was delivered to the area overlying the nasal bones and the septum. The pledgets were removed. Walsham forceps were used to reduce the nasal septum and nasal bones, and a Boies elevator was also used. The nasal bone fracture and septum were noted to be midline with normal anatomic contours. With proper anatomic reduction confirmed, Doyle splints were placed in bilateral nares and secured to the septum with a 2-0 silk tie. Quarter-inch iodoform packing was placed in the nasal-bone-reduction:side:0 nare beneath the fractured nasal bones for support and sutured to the nostril with 2-0 silk. An external Denver splint was placed over Steri-Strips.

title: Orbital Floor Reconstruction
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Orbital Floor Reconstruction

Attention was drawn to the orbital-floor-reconstruction:side:0 orbit, which was marked and grossly edematous. A forced duction test was performed showing a true restriction in upward gaze. Corneal shields with ophthalmic lubricant were placed in the orbital-floor-reconstruction:side:1 eye. An incision was marked on the lower lid approximately 7 mm below the lid margin in a skin crease, and 3 cc of 2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epi was infiltrated. The incision was made through skin and orbicularis oculi with a #15 blade, with a needle-tip Bovie used for hemostasis. Blunt dissection was carried to the inferior orbital rim, and at the level of periosteum a sharp incision was made to bone. A #9 periosteal elevator dissected and exposed the orbital floor and inferior orbital rim in a subperiosteal plane. The infraorbital nerve was protected throughout. The orbital floor defect was identified with orbital contents herniating into the maxillary sinus. The orbital contents were gently and atraumatically elevated back into the orbit, and stable native bone was exposed on all sides of the defect. The posterior stop measured orbital-floor-reconstruction:measurement:0 mm from the orbital rim. With the orbital tissues retracted superiorly, a pre-formed orbital floor reconstruction plate was measured, cut, and tried in for size, then adapted across the defect and perched on solid bone. Once all orbital contents were elevated superiorly, the plate was secured to the inferior orbital rim with orbital-floor-reconstruction:hardwareCount:0 self-drilling orbital-floor-reconstruction:measurement:1-mm screws and noted to be well seated and stable. The corneal shield was removed and a forced duction test showed a normal range of motion without restriction or impingement in any direction of gaze. The site was thoroughly irrigated with sterile saline and confirmed hemostatic. Periosteum was closed over the plate with several buried simple interrupted 5-0 Vicryl sutures, and the skin was closed with a running 4-0 plain gut suture. Ophthalmic sterile saline was used to irrigate the eye and a final forced duction test confirmed extraocular movements intact.

title: ORIF Le Fort
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: ORIF Le Fort

Lidocaine 1% with 1:200,000 epi was infiltrated into the maxillary vestibule. Attention was directed intraorally. A maxillary Keen incision was made 5 mm superior to the mucogingival junction with Bovie from first molar to first molar, with care not to disturb Stensen's duct, and carried through periosteum. A subperiosteal dissection was carried superiorly to expose the bilateral infraorbital foramina and lateral to the zygomaxillary sutures, exposing the Le Fort fractures bilaterally. The infraorbital nerves were visualized and protected throughout. The orif-le-fort:side:0 zygomaxillary fracture was confirmed properly reduced and stable, and fixated with a orif-le-fort:text:0 orif-le-fort:hardwareCount:0-hole L-plate using orif-le-fort:hardwareCount:1 orif-le-fort:measurement:0-mm screws. The contralateral zygomaticomaxillary fracture was fixated in identical fashion. The orif-le-fort:side:1 piriform rim was reduced and fixated with a orif-le-fort:text:1 orif-le-fort:hardwareCount:2-hole L-plate and orif-le-fort:hardwareCount:3 orif-le-fort:measurement:1-mm screws, and the contralateral piriform rim fixated in identical fashion. The maxilla was confirmed stable and the occlusion native and repeatable. All surgical sites were copiously irrigated with sterile saline and hemostasis confirmed. The intraoral incision was closed with 3-0 chromic gut in a running fashion.

PROCEDURE: ORIF Le Fort

Lidocaine 1% with 1:200,000 epi was infiltrated into the maxillary vestibule. Attention was directed intraorally. A maxillary Keen incision was made 5 mm superior to the mucogingival junction with Bovie from first molar to first molar, with care not to disturb Stensen's duct, and carried through periosteum. A subperiosteal dissection was carried superiorly to expose the bilateral infraorbital foramina and lateral to the zygomaxillary sutures, exposing the Le Fort fractures bilaterally. The infraorbital nerves were visualized and protected throughout. The [R/L] zygomaxillary fracture was confirmed properly reduced and stable, and fixated with a [profile] [#]-hole L-plate using [#] [length]-mm screws. The contralateral zygomaticomaxillary fracture was fixated in identical fashion. The [R/L] piriform rim was reduced and fixated with a [profile] [#]-hole L-plate and [#] [length]-mm screws, and the contralateral piriform rim fixated in identical fashion. The maxilla was confirmed stable and the occlusion native and repeatable. All surgical sites were copiously irrigated with sterile saline and hemostasis confirmed. The intraoral incision was closed with 3-0 chromic gut in a running fashion.

title: ORIF Mandible (body/angle/symphysis)
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: ORIF Mandible (body/angle/symphysis)

orif-mandible:hardwareCount:0 orif-mandible:measurement:0-mm intermaxillary fixation (IMF) screws were placed into the mandible and maxilla with even distribution (orif-mandible:hardwareCount:1 in the maxilla, orif-mandible:hardwareCount:2 in the mandible). Attention was directed to the orif-mandible:side:0 mandibular orif-mandible:enumText:0 fracture. A vestibular incision 5 mm below the mucogingival junction was made from tooth orif-mandible:toothNumber:0 and carried to bone with electrocautery, staying superior to the mental foramen. A subperiosteal dissection was performed with a #9 periosteal elevator. The mental foramen and mental nerve were visualized, isolated, skeletonized, and remained intact throughout the procedure. The fracture site was well exposed, including the inferior border, and the fracture was mobilized. The site was thoroughly irrigated with sterile saline. The patient was then placed tightly into maxillomandibular fixation (MMF) with 24-gauge wires looped between the IMF screws, and occlusion was verified to be native and stable. The fracture was reduced and confirmed satisfactory. A orif-mandible:text:0 orif-mandible:hardwareCount:3-hole straight plate was adapted equally across the fracture on the inferior border and secured with orif-mandible:hardwareCount:4 bicortical screws under copious irrigation. The fracture was re-evaluated and noted to be well reduced and stable, and the occlusion remained native. The MMF was released and occlusion and reduction were re-evaluated as stable, anatomic, and reproducible. All sites were irrigated with copious sterile saline and noted to be hemostatic. The mentalis was re-suspended with 3-0 Vicryl in a running fashion reinforced with simple interrupted 3-0 Vicryl, and the mucosa was re-approximated with 3-0 chromic gut in a running fashion. The patient was placed in guiding elastics.

title: ORIF NOE
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: ORIF NOE

Lidocaine 1% with 1:200,000 epi was infiltrated into the skin overlying the NOE complexes. Attention was directed to the nasofrontal junction and NOE fractures. Bilateral Lynch incisions were created in a natural skin crease, staying 1 cm away from the medial canthus bilaterally, using a 15 blade and carried to periosteum with Bovie electrocautery. Subperiosteal dissection exposed the comminuted NOE complex and fractures. For improved access, the Lynch incisions were connected via a horizontal incision across the nasofrontal junction crease (H-incision), and subperiosteal dissection completed to better expose the fractures. Hemostasis was achieved. The nasofrontal junction fracture was fixated with a orif-noe:text:0 orif-noe:hardwareCount:0-hole Y-plate and orif-noe:hardwareCount:1 orif-noe:measurement:0-mm screws under copious irrigation. The orif-noe:side:0 comminuted NOE fracture was reduced with a Boies elevator in the orif-noe:side:1 nare and fixated with a orif-noe:text:1 orif-noe:hardwareCount:2-hole curvilinear plate and orif-noe:hardwareCount:3 monocortical screws. Telescoping of the nasal bones was noted, reduced, and fixated with a orif-noe:text:2 orif-noe:hardwareCount:4-hole straight plate and orif-noe:hardwareCount:5 monocortical screws. Appropriate canthal width and nasal projection were achieved. The site was copiously irrigated and confirmed hemostatic. The H-incision was closed in multiple layers: 3-0 Vicryl to re-approximate muscle and cover the hardware in simple interrupted fashion, 4-0 Monocryl for the deep dermal layer in simple interrupted fashion, and 5-0 fast gut for the skin in a running fashion. The cutaneous incisions were cleaned and dressed with Dermabond and Steri-Strips. The nasal bones were re-evaluated and confirmed reduced, and an external nasal splint was placed.

title: ORIF ZMC
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: ORIF ZMC

Attention was directed to the orif-zmc:side:0 lateral brow, which was anesthetized with 2 cc of 1% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epi. Using a 15 blade, a curvilinear incision was made along the lateral orbital wall and subperiosteal dissection performed to expose the zygomaticofrontal suture, which was fractured and displaced. Attention was directed to the orif-zmc:side:1 maxillary vestibule, anesthetized with 3 cc of 1% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epi. Using a 15 blade, an incision was made 5 mm above the mucogingival junction from midline to the first molar region and carried to periosteum, and a subperiosteal dissection exposed the anterior maxillary sinus fracture and the zygomaticomaxillary suture fracture. The site was copiously irrigated. The ZF, ZM, and zygomatic arch fractures were manually reduced with Rowe disimpaction forceps. The ZF suture was fixated with a orif-zmc:text:0 orif-zmc:hardwareCount:0-hole curvilinear plate and self-drilling orif-zmc:measurement:0-mm screws. The ZM was fixated with a orif-zmc:text:1 L-plate secured with orif-zmc:hardwareCount:1 self-drilling orif-zmc:measurement:1-mm screws. Adequate reduction was confirmed. The infraorbital nerve was protected throughout. All surgical sites were irrigated with normal saline and hemostasis confirmed. The lateral brow incision was closed with 3-0 deep Vicryl, 4-0 subdermal Monocryl, and 5-0 plain gut for skin, with Steri-Strips placed over the incision. The intraoral incision was closed with 3-0 Vicryl in a running fashion.

title: Otoplasty
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Otoplasty

Attention was directed to the otoplasty:side:0 ear. Using a surgical marking pen, the proposed postauricular incision was designed with a otoplasty:measurement:0 mm width skin excision, and the kidney bean shaped piece of cartilage planned for excision was marked, preserving otoplasty:measurement:1 mm of conchal cartilage. 2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine was administered in the subcutaneous plane for hemostasis and hydrodissection. A surgical blade was used to create a linear incision through skin into subcutaneous tissue in the posterior auricular crease. Dissection was carried to the subperichondrial plane and the otoplasty:measurement:2 mm strip of skin was excised across the length of the incision. Sharp dissection exposed the conchal bowl and helix. Electrocautery was used to excise subcutaneous tissue in the mastoid region to establish a soft tissue bed of mastoid fascia for conchal bowl adaptation, and hemostasis was obtained. A 30 gauge hypodermic needle was passed through the cartilage from anterior to posterior to demarcate the base of the conchal bowl, and a kidney bean shaped piece of conchal bowl cartilage otoplasty:measurement:3 mm in width was excised, ensuring otoplasty:measurement:4 mm of conchal depth remained. 30 gauge needles were passed anterior to posterior to place Mustarde sutures in horizontal mattress fashion to recreate the antihelical fold, using two 4-0 Mersilene sutures. 3-0 Vicryl was used to suture the subperichondrial layer to the mastoid fascia in horizontal mattress and interrupted fashion. The lateral projection of the helix was verified with a ruler. The site was irrigated with copious normal saline, additional 3-0 Vicryl closed the subcutaneous layer, and the skin was closed with running 5-0 plain gut leaving otoplasty:measurement:5 mm at the apex for drainage. Blu-mousse was used to impress the conchal bowl as a pressure dressing with Xeroform placed in the conchal bowl, and Xeroform gauze was placed over the posterior site. The contralateral ear was completed in a similar fashion. A Barton headwrap was secured as dressing.

title: SARPE
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: SARPE

Attention was directed to the maxillary buccal vestibule. The maxillary surgical site was anesthetized with 10 cc of 2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine by local infiltration. A circumvestibular incision was initiated with bovie electrocautery from the left first molar approximately 5 mm superior to the mucogingival junction to the right first molar and carried down to bone. A full-thickness mucoperiosteal flap was reflected from the right maxillary buttress to the left maxillary buttress and superiorly to expose the piriform rims and the right and left infraorbital neurovascular bundles, both of which were intact and remained so for the duration of the case. The nasal mucosa was dissected off the bone bilaterally with a curved free elevator and a Sayer elevator. Bilateral Le Fort I osteotomy sites were marked with a reciprocating saw from the curvature of the maxillary buttress to the piriform rim in a flat, symmetric plane clear of the tooth roots, and completed with the saw, taking a conservative wedge of bone off the lateral zygomaticomaxillary junction to avoid interference with expansion. An interdental osteotomy was initiated between teeth sarpe:toothNumber:0 and sarpe:toothNumber:1 with an oscillating saw under copious irrigation. A double safe-sided osteotome was used to separate the septum from the palate and single safe-sided osteotomes were used to separate the lateral nasal walls. A straight osteotome completed the interdental osteotomy between sarpe:toothNumber:2 and sarpe:toothNumber:3, extended posteriorly to the posterior extent of the hard palate. The Hyrax appliance was activated sarpe:measurement:0 turns (sarpe:measurement:1 mm of expansion) to confirm symmetric expansion, and any interferences were conservatively reduced. The appliance was then reduced back to sarpe:measurement:2 turns (leaving sarpe:measurement:3 mm of expansion). The palatal soft tissues were inspected with no tears noted, and the segments were confirmed to move equally and independently. sarpe:optionalClause:0 The mucosa was tacked at the midline with a 3-0 chromic gut suture and the circumvestibular incision was closed bilaterally with running 3-0 chromic gut.

A full-thickness mucoperiosteal flap was reflected from the right maxillary buttress to the left maxillary buttress and superiorly to expose the piriform rims and the right and left infraorbital neurovascular bundles, both of which were intact and remained so for the duration of the case. The nasal mucosa was dissected off the bone bilaterally with a curved free elevator and a Sayer elevator. Bilateral Le Fort I osteotomy sites were marked with a reciprocating saw from the curvature of the maxillary buttress to the piriform rim in a flat, symmetric plane clear of the tooth roots, and completed with the saw, taking a conservative wedge of bone off the lateral zygomaticomaxillary junction to avoid interference with expansion. An interdental osteotomy was initiated between teeth [#__] and [#__] with an oscillating saw under copious irrigation. A double safe-sided osteotome was used to separate the septum from the palate and single safe-sided osteotomes were used to separate the lateral nasal walls. A straight osteotome completed the interdental osteotomy between [#__] and [#__], extended posteriorly to the posterior extent of the hard palate. The Hyrax appliance was activated [X] turns ([X] mm of expansion) to confirm symmetric expansion, and any interferences were conservatively reduced. The appliance was then reduced back to [X] turns (leaving [X] mm of expansion). The palatal soft tissues were inspected with no tears noted, and the segments were confirmed to move equally and independently. [Optional adjunct: bone morphogenetic protein (rhBMP-2), hydrated for 20 minutes on an absorbable collagen sponge, was placed into the interdental osteotomy after a final thorough irrigation.] The mucosa was tacked at the midline with a 3-0 chromic gut suture and the circumvestibular incision was closed bilaterally with running 3-0 chromic gut.

title: Septorhinoplasty
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Septorhinoplasty

The patient was anesthetized in the septum and bilateral turbinates with septorhinoplasty:measurement:0 cc of 2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine. Three pledgets soaked in Afrin were inserted into each nare. A Killian incision was made with a 15 blade through mucosa down to cartilage 1 cm posterior to the caudal septal margin. Subperichondrial dissection was performed with a Cottle to expose the entire left cartilaginous septum to the level of the maxillary crest and to the vomer posteriorly. Keeping a 10 mm dorsal and caudal strut, a 15 blade was used to incise through cartilage with care to keep the contralateral mucosa intact, and subperichondrial dissection was completed on the opposite side through the same incision. Deviated septal cartilage was harvested with septal scissors and Takahashi forceps. Deviated bony septum and spurs were addressed under endoscopic visualization with a Cottle, septal rongeur, and Takahashi forceps. Attention was then turned to the dorsum and tip as planned: through the septorhinoplasty:enumText:0 approach, dorsal hump reduction was performed with a septorhinoplasty:enumText:1, lateral and medial osteotomies were created with a septorhinoplasty:measurement:1 mm osteotome to narrow and straighten the bony pyramid, and tip refinement was achieved with [cephalic trim / interdomal and transdomal sutures / columellar strut] using septorhinoplasty:measurement:2-0 septorhinoplasty:text:0 suture. Harvested septal cartilage was used for grafting as needed. The septum was confirmed straight and the dorsal aesthetic lines symmetric. The Killian incision was closed with 4-0 chromic gut in interrupted and running fashion, gel foam was placed at the septoplasty site, and a 4-0 plain gut quilting stitch was placed through the septum. Doyle splints coated in bacitracin were inserted into each nare and secured to the septum with 2-0 silk. An external nasal splint was applied.

title: Sialolithotomy (Wharton Duct)
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Sialolithotomy (Wharton Duct)

The floor of the mouth was anesthetized with local infiltration of 2% lidocaine with epinephrine. The palpable stone in the sialolithotomy-wharton-duct:side:0 proximal hilum of Wharton's duct was localized by bimanual palpation. A trans-oral incision was made through the floor-of-mouth mucosa overlying the palpable stone with electrocautery, carried just through the mucosa. Blunt dissection was then carried out with a hemostat and Molt instruments, reflecting and protecting the lingual nerve and the sublingual gland, riding an avascular plane down to Wharton's duct and the stone. The lingual nerve was identified along the lateral aspect of the dissection and protected throughout. The sialolithotomy-wharton-duct:measurement:0 cm sialolith was visualized and carefully dissected free atraumatically, along with any additional smaller stones, and submitted in formalin for permanent pathologic examination. The duct was inspected and confirmed patent after stone removal, with salivary flow expressed. The surgical site was thoroughly irrigated with copious normal saline and hemostasis achieved. The lingual nerve and sublingual gland were confirmed intact and unaltered. The floor-of-mouth mucosa was loosely reapproximated with interrupted 3-0 chromic gut sutures to allow for adequate drainage. Specimen: sialolithotomy-wharton-duct:text:0 to permanent pathology.

title: TMJ Arthroscopy (Lysis and Lavage with PRF)
sourcePath: Note Templates/Procedures/TMJ/TMJ Arthroscopy Lysis Lavage PRF.md
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: TMJ Arthroscopy (Lysis and Lavage with PRF)

Prior to induction, peripheral blood was drawn into PRF tubes and centrifuged per standard protocol to yield injectable PRF (i-PRF). The patient was placed supine and underwent nasal endotracheal intubation without incident. The tmj-arthroscopy-lysis-lavage-prf:side:0 face and ear were prepped and draped in standard sterile fashion, and a cotton pledget was placed in the tmj-arthroscopy-lysis-lavage-prf:side:1 external auditory meatus.

The canthal-tragal line and articular fossa landmarks were marked. The tmj-arthroscopy-lysis-lavage-prf:side:2 superior joint space was insufflated with tmj-arthroscopy-lysis-lavage-prf:measurement:0 cc of Lactated Ringer's using a 27-gauge needle, which returned freely, confirming intracapsular placement. A stab incision was made over the posterior puncture site, and the trocar and cannula were introduced into the superior joint space. The arthroscope was introduced and a diagnostic sweep was performed. An anterior portal was established using a triangulation technique. Thorough lysis of adhesions within the anterior recess and superior joint space was performed with blunt instrumentation, and the joint was copiously lavaged with approximately tmj-arthroscopy-lysis-lavage-prf:measurement:1 cc of Lactated Ringer's.

Following successful lysis and lavage, the prepared liquid i-PRF (approximately tmj-arthroscopy-lysis-lavage-prf:measurement:2 cc) was injected into the superior joint space and retrodiscal tissues under direct visualization. The instruments were withdrawn and hemostasis was achieved with manual pressure. A single 6-0 Prolene suture was placed at the trocar site. The external auditory meatus pledget was removed and the ear was examined.

title: Discopexy (Mitek Anchor)
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Discopexy (Mitek Anchor)

The patient was placed supine and underwent nasal endotracheal intubation without incident. The patient was prepped and draped in standard sterile fashion and the tmj-discopexy-mitek-anchor:side:0 external auditory canal was protected with a Betadine-soaked kittner. An Ioban was placed over the oral cavity to isolate the sterile field.

Attention was directed to the tmj-discopexy-mitek-anchor:side:1 TMJ. The endaural incision was marked and infiltrated with 3 cc of 2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine. Using a #15 blade, the endaural incision was made through dermis to subcutaneous tissue and continued anteriorly following the tragal cartilage. Blunt dissection continued above the zygomatic arch to identify the temporalis fascia, where a circumferential plane was created and connected to the avascular plane below the zygomatic arch. The zygomatic arch was identified and Bovie electrocautery was used to incise the periosteum. Subperiosteal dissection was carried over the arch and the lateral joint capsule was visualized with a #9 Molt elevator.

The superior joint space was insufflated with 1 cc of 2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine. An incision was made between the lateral joint space and the glenoid fossa to enter the superior joint space, which was explored with a Freer and freed of adhesions from the meniscus to the glenoid fossa and articular eminence. A #15 blade was used to incise over the condylar head to enter the inferior joint space, which was explored. The disc was tmj-discopexy-mitek-anchor:optionalClause:0 displaced without perforation, with hyperemia and inflammation of the retrodiscal tissue. A curved Freer was used to free the articular disc from the glenoid and condyle and reposition it over the condyle. Inflamed retrodiscal tissue was excised.

A Mitek anchor was placed within the posterior-lateral aspect of the condyle. The attached braided suture was secured to the medial and posterior aspects of the articular disc. The mandible was manipulated to confirm appropriate disc position with the condyle in function. The surgical site was copiously irrigated with sterile saline and confirmed hemostatic.

PROCEDURE: Discopexy (Mitek Anchor)

The patient was placed supine and underwent nasal endotracheal intubation without incident. The zygomatic arch was identified and Bovie electrocautery was used to incise the periosteum. The superior joint space was insufflated with 1 cc of 2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine. An incision was made between the lateral joint space and the glenoid fossa to enter the superior joint space, which was explored with a Freer and freed of adhesions from the meniscus to the glenoid fossa and articular eminence. A #15 blade was used to incise over the condylar head to enter the inferior joint space, which was explored. The disc was [medially and anteriorly] displaced without perforation, with hyperemia and inflammation of the retrodiscal tissue. A curved Freer was used to free the articular disc from the glenoid and condyle and reposition it over the condyle. Inflamed retrodiscal tissue was excised.

A Mitek anchor was placed within the posterior-lateral aspect of the condyle. The attached braided suture was secured to the medial and posterior aspects of the articular disc. The mandible was manipulated to confirm appropriate disc position with the condyle in function. The surgical site was copiously irrigated with sterile saline and confirmed hemostatic.

title: Total Joint Replacement (TMJ)
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Total Joint Replacement (TMJ)

The patient was placed supine, secured with a security strap, and SCDs were placed with pressure points padded. General anesthesia was induced and nasal endotracheal intubation was achieved. A head wrap was used to protect the scalp and secure the tube, the eyes were lubricated and taped closed, and otoscopic exam confirmed intact tympanic membranes. A throat pack was placed and the mouth was disinfected with chlorhexidine. tmj-total-joint-replacement:hardwareCount:0 tmj-total-joint-replacement:measurement:0-mm IMF screws were placed in the maxilla and mandible in an evenly distributed manner. The head and neck were prepped with Betadine and an Ioban was placed over the mouth to maintain a sterile field. The bilateral TMJ surgical sites were irrigated with vancomycin solution throughout the procedure.

Attention was directed to the tmj-total-joint-replacement:side:0 TMJ. The endaural incision was infiltrated with 2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine and a #15 blade was used to incise from the earlobe to the root of the helix. Sharp dissection proceeded anterior to the tragal cartilage in the supraperichondrial plane to the zygomatic arch, and the periosteum was incised with subperiosteal dissection to the anterior arch. The lateral capsule was incised and the condylar head visualized. The lateral condyle and ramus were bluntly dissected in a subperiosteal plane. A tmj-total-joint-replacement:side:1 retromandibular/submandibular incision was then performed: a #15 blade was used through skin and platysma, with blunt dissection to the angle deep to the parotid, the marginal mandibular branch protected superiorly, and a facial nerve stimulator at 0.5 mA used throughout. The pterygomasseteric sling was incised and subperiosteal dissection connected the preauricular and retromandibular spaces.

The condylar cutting guide was placed and tmj-total-joint-replacement:measurement:1 mm of condylar height was removed with the Piezo; the osteotomy was completed with a T-handle osteotome and the condyle removed. The disc was separated from the lateral pterygoid and retrodiscal tissue with electrocautery, and the condyle and disc were submitted for histologic analysis. A lateral ramus reduction guide was placed and the osseous recontouring performed with a power rasp and copious irrigation to create a flat lateral surface. Fossa reduction and drill guides were used to recontour the articular eminence and pre-drill the fossa holes. A tmj-total-joint-replacement:enumText:0 fossa component was placed and a stable fit confirmed with matching of the pre-drilled holes, then secured with tmj-total-joint-replacement:hardwareCount:1 tmj-total-joint-replacement:measurement:2-mm screws.

The lateral ramus was contoured to fit the condylar component. A tmj-total-joint-replacement:enumText:1 condylar component was placed through the neck incision and seated into the fossa component with the mandible fully seated, then secured with tmj-total-joint-replacement:hardwareCount:2 tmj-total-joint-replacement:measurement:3-mm bicortical screws.

The same procedure was performed on the contralateral side as indicated by the operative plan, with placement of the tmj-total-joint-replacement:enumText:2 fossa and condylar components secured with tmj-total-joint-replacement:hardwareCount:3 tmj-total-joint-replacement:measurement:4-mm screws.

The mouth was entered through the Ioban and MMF was completed. The occlusion was confirmed stable and coincident with the pre-operative plan, then released and re-verified. Abdominal fat was harvested from an infraumbilical incision (approximately tmj-total-joint-replacement:measurement:5 cm block) and tmj-total-joint-replacement:measurement:6 cc was packed around each prosthesis through the endaural incisions under vancomycin irrigation; the abdominal donor site was closed in layers and dressed with Dermabond.

The pterygomasseteric sling and platysma were re-approximated with 3-0 Vicryl, the deep dermal layer closed with 4-0 Monocryl, and the skin closed with tmj-total-joint-replacement:enumText:3. Incisions were dressed with Dermabond. The Ioban and throat pack were removed, the occlusion re-verified, guiding elastics placed as indicated, and a JawBra compression dressing applied.

title: Upper Blepharoplasty
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Upper Blepharoplasty

The patient was marked in a seated, upright position prior to the procedure. The inferior limb of the incision was marked within the natural supratarsal crease, approximately upper-blepharoplasty:measurement:0 mm above the ciliary margin. The superior limb was marked using a pinch test to identify redundant skin, ensuring at least upper-blepharoplasty:measurement:1 mm of skin remained between the inferior brow margin and the ciliary margin for appropriate closure. Corneal shields were placed bilaterally after application of ophthalmic lubricating ointment. Local anesthesia consisting of 1% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine was infiltrated into the planned surgical sites. Following appropriate time for hemostasis, attention was directed to the right upper eyelid. A #15 blade was used to incise along the previously marked lines. A strip of redundant skin and a conservative strip of the underlying orbicularis oculi muscle were excised using Westcott scissors. The orbital septum was carefully opened medially and the medial fat pad was identified. The fat pad was gently teased out, clamped, excised, and the stump cauterized with bipolar electrocautery before retracting back into the orbit. Meticulous hemostasis was achieved throughout the surgical bed with bipolar electrocautery. The incision was closed with a 6-0 Prolene suture in a running continuous fashion. The identical procedure was performed on the contralateral upper eyelid and bilateral symmetry was assessed and noted to be excellent. The corneal shields were removed, the eyes were cleaned, and erythromycin ophthalmic ointment was applied to the suture lines.
